revision.text:
[[de:Hibis]] {{Infobox hieroglyphs |NAME = O4 D58-X1:O49 |NAME-TRANSCRIPTION = Ḥebet
''Ḥbt'' |GREEK = Ἱβιτῶν πόλις |COPTIC = Hēb
ϨⲎⲂ |IMAGE1 = HibisFromNadura.jpg |IMAGE1-WIDTH = 290px |IMAGE1-CAPTION = View of the Temple of Hibis from the upper temple of an‐Nadura. Parts of ancient Hibis are today covered by palm gardens. }} '''Hibis''' ({{ill|Ancient Egyptian|ja|古代エジプト語|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to エジプト語|1=Egyptian language|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q50868}} ''Ḥbt'' “Plow City”, {{laS|Hibeos}}, {{grcS|Ἱβις, Ἱβιτῶν πόλις, Ἥβις}}, {{ill|Coptic language|en|Coptic language|qid=Q36155|lt=Coptic language|1=Coptic language}} ϨⲎⲂ, ''Hēb'', {{arS|هيبس‎|DMG=Hībis}})For the Nemes attestations, see: {{Literature |Author=Guy Wagner |Title=Les oasis d’Égypte: à l’époque grecque, romaine et byzantine d’après les documents grecs |Series=Bibliothèque d’étude |Volume=100 |Publisher=L'Institut français d’archéologie orientale |Place=Cairo |Date=1987 |ISBN=2-7247-0050-3 |Pages=155–157}} – {{Literature |Author=Werner Vycichl |Title=Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue copte |Publisher=Peeters |Place=Leuven |Date=1983 |Pages=288}} was a {{ill|metropolis|ja|大都市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/metropolis|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 都市|1=City|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q515}} in ancient {{ill|Egypt|en|Egypt|qid=Q79|lt=Egypt|1=Egypt}} in the north of the {{ill|basin|ja|盆地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/basin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Basin (geomorphology)|qid=Q813672}} of {{ill|Kharga Oasis|lt=al-Charga|ja|アル＝チャルガ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kharga Oasis|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Architectural remains and finds show that the city existed at least since the {{ill|Late Period|ja|後期|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and was inhabited into {{ill|Roman|ja|ローマ時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Roman|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 古代ローマ|1=Ancient Rome|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1747689}}–{{ill|Coptic church|ja|コプト教会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Coptic church|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コプト正教会|1=Coptic Orthodox Church|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q198998}} times. Among its most important legacies are the {{ill|Temple of Hibis|ja|ヒビス神殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temple of Hibis|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which was decorated under the Persian king {{ill|Darius the Great|en|Darius the Great|qid=Q44387|lt=Darius I|1=Darius the Great}} and dedicated to the gods {{ill|Amun-Ra|ja|アメン・ラー|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amun-Ra|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Mut|ja|ムート|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mut|1=ムート|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q65243029}} and {{ill|Khonsu|en|Khonsu|qid=Q190521|lt=Khonsu|1=Khonsu}}, as well as the ancient settlement area {{ill|Ain al-Charab|ja|アイン・アル＝シャラブ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ain al-Charab|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Ain at‐Turba). == Location and Significance == The metropolis and current archaeological site is located about 1.5 km north of the modern town {{ill|Kharga Oasis|lt=al-Charga|ja|アル＝チャルガ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kharga Oasis|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in the eponymous basin and south of the Roman–Christian cemetery {{ill|Gabbānat al-Bagawāt|ar|جبانة البجوات‎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gabbānat al-Bagawāt|qq=}} on the southern spur of {{arS|جبل الطير‎|DMG=Ǧabal aṭ-Ṭair|de=Vogelberg}}. The exact extent of the former metropolis remains unknown since no comprehensive excavations have yet been carried out. The roughly one–square–kilometer city probably extended in the east as far as the hill of an–Nāḍūra ({{arS|الناضورة‎}}), on which a temple dedicated to the god Khonsu is situated, in the west to the southern foothills of {{arS|جبل تارف‎}} (Jabal Tārif), and in the south into the area of the modern town {{ill|Kharga Oasis|lt=al-Charga|ja|アル＝チャルガ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kharga Oasis|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.H. E. Winlock, N. de Garis Davies: ''The Temple of Hibis in el Khargeh Oasis.'' Vol. 1, New York 1938, Plate XXIX. At the center of the city is the Temple of Hibis, which was situated to the west of a large ancient lake that belonged to the area of ancient Hibis. North of the temple is the archaeological site ʿAin al-Charāb ({{arS|عين الخراب‎|de=Ruinenquelle}}), also called ʿAin at‐Turba ({{arS|عين التربة‎}}), with its settlement remains and rock–cut tombs. The city held important geostrategic significance and developed into a trading center. The ancient caravan route “Darb al-Arbaʿīn” ({{arS|درب الاربعين‎|de=Piste der vierzig [Tage]}}) from {{ill|Asyut|ja|アスユート|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asyut|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アシュート|1=Asyut|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q29962}} to {{ill|Kobbe|ja|コッベ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobbe|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in the district of {{ill|Darfur|en|Darfur|qid=Q46733|lt=Darfur|1=Darfur}} in the {{ill|Sudan|en|Sudan|qid=Q1049|lt=Sudan|1=Sudan}} passed through it. To the north, the track “Darb ʿAin Amūr” ({{arS|درب عين أمور‎}}) led along to {{ill|ad-Dachla|ja|アド・ダクラ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ad-Dachla|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which had been used since the {{ill|Old Kingdom|ja|古王国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Old Kingdom|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to エジプト古王国|1=Old Kingdom of Egypt|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q177819}}.{{Literature |Author={{ill|Salima Ikram|ja|サリマ・イクラム|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Salima Ikram|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Corinna Rossi |Title=An Early Dynastic serekh from the Kharga Oasis |Series=The journal of Egyptian archaeology |Volume=90 |Date=2004 |Pages=211–215}} == History == Archaeological evidence for the Old and {{ill|Middle Kingdom|ja|中王国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Middle Kingdom|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in the al‐Charga basin is so far scant.See the aforementioned serekh, archaeological site of {{ill|Umm Mawagir|ja|ウム・マワギル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umm Mawagir|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Administratively, the basin belonged at least during the {{ill|New Kingdom|ja|新王国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Kingdom|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to the 8th Upper Egyptian {{ill|district|ja|地区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/district|1=chiku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q16282564}}.{{Literature |Editor=Elke Blumenthal et al. |Title=Section 4: Documents of the 18th Dynasty; translation of fascicles 5–16 |Publisher=Akademie-Verlag |Place=Berlin |Date=1984 |Pages=356 (Document 280 A, 963), p. 365 (Document 283.h)}} It is assumed that the Temple of Hibis was erected already in the {{ill|26th Dynasty|ja|第26王朝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/26th Dynasty|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the so–called Saitic Period. It is conceivable that the temple was built on the site of an earlier structure since corresponding fragments were found during the excavations.This assumption is supported, inter alia, by fragments of a sacrificial bowl bearing the name of the king {{ill|Apries|ja|アプリーズ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Apries|1=APPLIESE|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11284750}}, see H. E. Winlock, N. de Garis Davies: ''The Temple of Hibis in el Khargeh Oasis.'' Vol. 1, New York 1938, pp. 39, 41, Plate XXVI. A, B. The decoration of the temple was only carried out in Persian times under {{ill|Darius the Great|en|Darius the Great|qid=Q44387|lt=Darius I|1=Darius the Great}}Darius I also had the sanctum at the temple of Qasr al-Ghuwaita decorated. and his successor {{ill|Darius II|en|Darius II|qid=Q202236|lt=Darius II|1=Darius II}}. Further additions and decorations were made under {{ill|Hakor|en|Hakor|qid=Q360012|lt=Hakor|1=Hakor}}, {{ill|Nectanebo I|ja|ネクタネboス1世|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nectanebo I|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Nectanebo II|ja|ネクタネboス2世|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nectanebo II|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and by {{ill|Ptolemaic dynasty|en|Ptolemaic dynasty|qid=Q131976|lt=Ptolemaic|1=Ptolemaic dynasty}} kings. In Roman times, Hibis was the seat of a Roman {{ill|strategos|ja|ストラテゴス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/strategos|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 将軍職 (アテナイ)|1=Strategos|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q335954}} (district governor), who had edicts posted at the gates of the Temple of Hibis. The earliest edict, that of Gnaeus Vergilius Capito, was issued in AD 49 by the strategos Posidonios. The most recent and significant edict comes from the prefect {{ill|Tiberius Julius Alexander|ja|ティベリウス・ユリウス・アレクサンダー|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tiberius Julius Alexander|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and was posted in AD 68 by the strategos Iulius Demetrius. It deals with economic and fiscal matters.{{Literature |Author=André Bernand |Title=La prose sur pierre: dans l’Égypte hellénistique et romaine |Publisher=Ed. du Centre National de la Recherche Scientifique |Place=Paris |Date=1992 |ISBN=2-222-04695-5 |Comment=No. 53–57, number 57 contains the edict of Tiberius Julius Alexander}} The temple was used until the rise of Christianity at the end of the 4th century. Still in the 3rd century, Hermeias, son of Hermophilus from {{ill|Hermopolis Magna|ja|ヘルモポリス・マグナ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hermopolis Magna|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, had new stone paving installed.H. E. Winlock, N. de Garis Davies: ''The Temple of Hibis in el Khargeh Oasis.'' Vol. 1, New York 1938, p. 37, Plate XXX. The settlement of Ain al-Charab may have existed into the 4th century AD. In the settlement area, frescoes with Christian motifs were discovered.[http://www.sis.gov.eg/En/Story.aspx?sid=63656 ''Archaeological mural on Christ’s miracles discovered in New Valley''], news of 2 September 2012 on Egypt Online. The tombs in the east of Ain al-Charab may date back to the New Kingdom. == Research History == The early 19th century is regarded as a great era of discoveries in the {{ill|Libyan Desert|en|Libyan Desert|qid=Q181528|lt=Libyan Desert|1=Libyan Desert}} (Western Desert). The Frenchman {{ill|Frédéric Cailliaud|ja|フレデリック・カイリオ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Frédéric Cailliaud|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} discovered the Temple of Hibis in 1818.{{Literature |Author=Frédéric Cailliaud |Title=Voyage à l’oasis de Thèbes et dans les déserts situés à l’orient et à l’occident de la Thébaïde: fait pendant les années 1815, 1816, 1817 et 1818 |Volume=1 |Publisher=Imprimerie Royale |Place=Paris |Date=1821 |Pages=88–95 |Source=Tf. x–xxiii}} He was followed by {{ill|Archibald Edmonstone|ja|アーチボルド・エドモンストン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Archibald Edmonstone|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1819),{{Literature |Author=Archibald Edmonstone |Title=A journey to two of the oases of Upper Egypt |Publisher=Murray |Place=London |Date=1822 |Pages=60–74}} the Britons {{ill|John Gardner Wilkinson|ja|ジョン・ガードナー・ウィルキンソン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/John Gardner Wilkinson|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1825){{Literature |Author=John Gardner Wilkinson |Title=Modern Egypt and Thebes: being a description of Egypt, including the information required for travellers in that country |Volume=2 |Publisher=Murray |Place=London |Date=1843 |Pages=366–371}} and {{ill|George Alexander Hoskins|ja|ジョージ・アレクサンダー・ホスキンス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/George Alexander Hoskins|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1832){{Literature |Author=George Alexander Hoskins |Title=Visit to the great Oasis of the Libyan desert |Publisher=Longman |Place=London |Date=1837}}. The German {{ill|Heinrich Brugsch|ja|ハインリッヒ・ブルックス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heinrich Brugsch|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} presented the first scientific description of the Temple of Hibis.{{Literature |Author=Heinrich Brugsch |Title=Reise nach der großen Oase El Khargeh in der Libyschen Wüste |Publisher=Hinrichs |Place=Leipzig |Date=1878 |Online=[http://digi.ub.uni-heidelberg.de/diglit/brugsch1878 digi.ub.uni-heidelberg.de]}} A more extensive investigation of the Temple of Hibis and of Ain at-Turba was only carried out by the American Egyptologists {{ill|Herbert E. Winlock|ja|ハーバート・E・ウィンロック|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Herbert E. Winlock|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Norman de Garis Davies|ja|ノーマン・デ・ガリス・デイヴィス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Norman de Garis Davies|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Albert M. Lythgoe|ja|アルバート・M・リスグ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Albert M. Lythgoe|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in the years 1909–1913 and 1926–1939, who conducted and documented their excavations as part of the “Egyptian Expedition” for the {{ill|Metropolitan Museum of Art|en|Metropolitan Museum of Art|qid=Q160236|lt=Metropolitan Museum of Art|1=Metropolitan Museum of Art}} in {{ill|New York City|en|New York City|qid=Q60|lt=New York|1=New York City}}. In the 1980s, the inscriptions of the Temple of Hibis were re–examined and published by the Canadian Egyptologist {{ill|Eugene Cruz-Uribe|ja|ユージーン・クルス＝ウリベ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eugene Cruz-Uribe|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Within the framework of the investigations of the cemetery el–Bagawāt by the “Egyptian Expedition”, ʿAin al-Charab/ʿAin at-Turba was briefly examined in 1907/1908{{Literature |Author=Albert M. Lythgoe |Title=The Oasis of Kharga |Series=The Metropolitan Museum of Art Bulletin |Volume=3 |Number=11 |Date=1908-11 |ISSN=0026-1521 |Pages=203–208, especially p. 208 |JSTOR=3253214}}{{Literature |Author=Albert M. Lythgoe |Title=The Egyptian Expedition |Series=The Metropolitan Museum of Art Bulletin |Volume=4 |Number=7 |Date=1909-07 |ISSN=0026-1521 |Pages=119–123 |Source=Especially pp. 121–123, Fig. 4, 7 |JSTOR=3252459}} and in 1930/1931{{Literature |Author={{ill|Walter Hauser|ja|ヴァルター・ハウザー|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Walter Hauser|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |Title=The Christian Necropolis in Khargeh Oasis |Series=The Metropolitan Museum of Art Bulletin |Volume=27 |Number=3, Part 2: ''The Egyptian Expedition 1930–1931.'' |Date=1932-03 |ISSN=0026-1521 |Pages=38–50 |Source=Especially p. 38 |Online=[http://www.metmuseum.org/pubs/bulletins/1/pdf/3255361.pdf.bannered.pdf metmuseum.org] |Format=PDF}} The settlement area and the rock–cut tomb cemetery were examined several times from 1986 to 1999 by the Egyptian Antiquities Service, the {{ill|Supreme Council of Antiquities|ja|古代遺産最高評議会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Supreme Council of Antiquities|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Since 2007 the settlement area has been systematically researched under the direction of {{ill|Manṣūr 'Osmān|ja|マンシュール・オスマーン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manṣūr 'Osmān|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.Personal communication with the head of the Egyptian Antiquities Service in al‐Charga, Bahgat Ahmed Ibrahim. The results of all these excavations have so far been scarcely published. == Monuments == === Temple of Hibis === [[File:HibisComplete.jpg|thumb|Temple of Hibis, as seen from the southeast]] The Amun temple of Hibis was established on the west bank of an ancient lake for the Theban divine triad of Amun, Lord of Hibis, Mut and Khonsu, and has been enclosed by a stone wall since the time of {{ill|Nectanebo I|ja|ネクタネボス1世|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nectanebo I|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} or {{ill|Nectanebo II|ja|ネクタネボス2世|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nectanebo II|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The temple was connected with the Kaian complex by means of a sphinx avenue, which was only constructed in Ptolemaic times. Along the avenue there are three gate passages. The two eastern passages were only built in Ptolemaic times and were provided with edicts in Roman times. The third gate passage features depictions of {{ill|Darius|en|Darius|qid=Q121627|lt=Darius|1=Darius}}; on one hand he is shown presenting an image of {{ill|Maat|en|Maat|qid=Q170471|lt=Maat|1=Maat}} to {{ill|Amun-Ra|ja|アメン・ラー|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amun-Ra|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Mut|ja|ムート|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mut|1=ムート|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q65243029}}, and on the other hand he sacrifices lettuce to {{ill|Amun-Ra|ja|アメン・ラー|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amun-Ra|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The sandstone temple, measuring 19 × 44 meters, consists of a {{ill|portico|ja|ポルティコ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/portico|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ポルチコ|1=Portico|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q185600}}, two {{ill|column halls|ja|柱の間|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/column halls|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (from {{ill|hypostyle|ja|ヒポスタイル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hypostyle|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), several magazine rooms, the {{ill|altar hall|ja|祭壇ホール|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/altar hall|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the {{ill|sanctuary|ja|聖域|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sanctuary|1=seiiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11610956}}. On the temple roof there is a roof temple dedicated to the god {{ill|Osiris|en|Osiris|qid=Q46491|lt=Osiris|1=Osiris}}. The portico displays depictions of {{ill|Nectanebo II|ja|ネクタネボス2世|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nectanebo II|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} performing various rituals before the gods. The following, the first column hall, is decorated only on its back wall: {{ill|Darius|en|Darius|qid=Q121627|lt=Darius|1=Darius}} is shown in various sacrificial acts before the gods, including before {{ill|Amun-Ra|ja|アメン・ラー|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amun-Ra|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Mut|ja|ムート|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mut|1=ムート|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q65243029}} and {{ill|Khonsu|en|Khonsu|qid=Q190521|lt=Khonsu|1=Khonsu}}. On the right rear wall is the famous depiction of the falcon–headed, winged {{ill|Set (deity)|en|Set (deity)|qid=Q131795|lt=Seth|1=Set (deity)}}, who kills the serpent {{ill|Apophis (disambiguation)|en|Apophis (disambiguation)|qid=Q396228|lt=Apophis|1=Apophis (disambiguation)}}, the arch–enemy of the god {{ill|Ra|en|Ra|qid=Q1252904|lt=Ra|1=Ra}}, with a lance. The second column hall again shows King Darius performing sacrificial acts. This hall is significant in the history of religion. It contains three hymns to the creator god {{ill|Amun|en|Amun|qid=Q58373|lt=Amun|1=Amun}}. The sanctuary contains a list of approximately 700 depictions of deities as well as the creator god {{ill|Ra|en|Ra|qid=Q1252904|lt=Ra|1=Ra}} in various manifestations. === Settlement ʿAin al-Charab === [[File:AinKharabSettlement.jpg|thumb|Settlement ʿAin al-Charab, view to the west]] From the settlement in the west, numerous houses and street networks have already been uncovered. The houses were constructed of mud bricks and once featured [[:en:barrel vaults|barrel vaults]] ceilings. The arrangement of the rooms was irregular. The walls were plastered and whitened. Some houses still bore remnants of {{ill|frescoes|en|frescoes|qid=Q19717841|lt=frescoes|1=Frescoes}} on their interior walls. Among the finds of the ''Egyptian Expedition'' from 1908 were undecorated and decorated ceramics, glazed and glass beads, ornaments, a plaster statuette, coins from the period between Emperor {{ill|Diocletian|en|Diocletian|qid=Q43107|lt=Diocletian|1=Diocletian}} (reign 284–305), Emperor {{ill|Maximian|en|Maximian|qid=Q46768|lt=Maximian|1=Maximian}} (reign 286–305) and {{ill|Constantine the Great|ja|コンスタンティヌス大帝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Constantine the Great|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コンスタンティヌス1世|1=Constantine the Great|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q8413}} (reign 306–337) as well as various glass objects. Part of the glassware belonged to the multicolored so–called {{ill|Millefiori Glasses|ja|ミッレフィオリガラス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Millefiori Glasses|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (thread glasses). Another glass cup fragment, 2.7 centimeters in size, depicts a tiger tearing apart an antelope.Acc. no. 15.1.1. “See:” {{Literature |Author=Christine Alexander |Title=Accessions of Greek and Roman Antiquities |Series=The Metropolitan Museum of Art Bulletin |Volume=32 |Number=7 |Date=1937-07 |ISSN=0026-1521 |Pages=175–177 |Source=Especially p. 176 f., Fig. 3 |Online=[http://www.metmuseum.org/pubs/bulletins/1/pdf/3255076.pdf.bannered.pdf metmuseum.org] |Format=PDF}} – [http://www.metmuseum.org/pubs/bulletins/1/pdf/3255077.pdf.bannered.pdf Rest of the article] (PDF; 877 kB). In the most recent excavations after 2007, a wall painting was also discovered that shows Jesus healing the sick. In the east of the settlement there are several rock–cut tombs which, according to the ceramic finds, may date back to the New Kingdom. The tombs themselves are undecorated and partly contain internal pillars. == Literature == '''Temple of Hibis''' * {{ill|Herbert E. Winlock|ja|ハーバート・E・ウィンロック|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Herbert E. Winlock|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Norman de Garis Davies|ja|ノーマン・デ・ガリス・デイヴィス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Norman de Garis Davies|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''The Temple of Hibis in el Khargeh Oasis.'' 3 volumes. Metropolitan Museum of Art, Egyptian Expedition, New York 1938–1953. * {{Literature |Author={{ill|Eugene Cruz-Uribe|ja|ユージーン・クルス＝ウリベ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eugene Cruz-Uribe|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |Title=Hibis Temple Project |Volume=Volume 1: ''Translations, commentary, discussions and sign list'' |Publisher=Van Siclen |Place=San Antonio (Tex.) |Date=1988 |ISBN=0-933175-14-0}} '''Hymns to the Creator God Amun-Ra''' * {{Literature |Author={{ill|Jan Assmann|ja|ヤン・アスマン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jan Assmann|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |Title=Egyptian Hymns and Prayers |Series=Orbis biblicus et orientalis |Edition=2nd, revised and expanded |Publisher=Universitätsverlag |Place=Freiburg (Switzerland) |Date=1999 |ISBN=3-525-53649-6 |Reference=''Hymns.'' pp. 128–130}} * {{Literature |Author={{ill|David Klotz|ja|デイビッド・クロッツ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/David Klotz|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |Title=Adoration of the Ram. Five Hymns to Amun-Ra from Hibis Temple |Series=Yale Egyptological Studies |Volume=6 |Publisher=Yale Egyptological Seminar |Place=New Haven (Conn.) |Date=2006 |ISBN=0-9740025-2-6}} == External Links == {{Wikivoyage|Hībis}} {{Wikivoyage|ʿAin el-Charāb}} == References == {{Coordinate |NS=25/28/36/N |EW=30/33/21/E |type=landmark |region=EG}} {{translated page|de|Hibis|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} [[de:Hibis]] {{wikidata link|Q1617203}} [[Category:Archaeological sites in Africa]] [[Category:Archaeological sites in Egypt]] [[Category:Municipalities of ancient Egypt]] [[Category:New Valley Governorate]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]]
